title: Agent Control Contract — Live Event Stream (agent://events read body)
description: The read body of the `agent://events` resource: a subscribable, bounded live-event window an operator drains to tail activity — loop steps, refusals, spawns, drain progress — over the self-MCP, without scraping container stderr (agent RFC 0016 §7). It is a PROJECTION of the same stderr JSON-lines stream (RFC 0010 §3.2/§3.3), not a second telemetry path: identical lines, identical closed event vocabulary, plus a monotonic ring `seq`. Backed by a fixed-size in-memory ring (env AGENT_EVENTS_RING; default 1024) that is LOSSY BY DESIGN — an overrun drops the oldest and bumps `dropped`, never blocking a slow subscriber. The transport is RFC 0005 notify-then-read: a payload-free `notifications/resources/updated{uri}` fires on new events; the subscriber `resources/read("agent://events?after=<seq>")` to pull the window with seq > after, optionally filtered by `?level=` and `?event=<dotted-prefix-comma-list>`, advancing `after` to the last seq it saw. The ring is in-memory and dropped wholesale on SIGKILL; the durable record is stderr + the run report. This envelope is OPEN/additive-tolerant.

x-contract.owner_rfc: agent RFC 0016 §7 (envelope); RFC 0010 §3.2/§3.3 (line schema + event vocabulary)
x-contract.manifest_surface: surfaces.events_schema (presence gated by surfaces.events; feature `events` + a served self-MCP)
x-contract.versioning: events_schema major.minor versions ONLY the envelope (oldest_seq/newest_seq/dropped/events shape), NOT the line schema (RFC 0010 owns and versions that). A new event NAME is additive (consumer tolerates it). A new line field is additive. The consumer refuses only an unknown events_schema MAJOR. Surface absent => no ring built => consumer degrades to stderr/log scraping where reachable.

read_request.notes: after defaults to 0 (whole held window). Unknown query keys are ignored (forward-compatible). A malformed `after` falls back to 0. `event` is a comma-list of dotted PREFIXES matched against the line `event` (e.g. `subagent.,limit.`). The server caps one read window (reference impl: 512 entries); the subscriber pages by advancing `after`.

events_schema.description: Envelope schema version major.minor (RFC 0016 §7.2/§8.1). Current 1.0. Versions the envelope shape only.

oldest_seq.description: Smallest ring `seq` currently held. If a subscriber's `after` predates this, it fell behind (see `dropped`) and must re-baseline.

newest_seq.description: Largest ring `seq` currently held. The subscriber advances its cursor toward this.

dropped.description: Cumulative count of lines evicted from the ring since process start (monotonic). dropped > 0 since the last read means the subscriber fell behind and lost lines — lossy by design (RFC 0016 §7.2/§8.4).

events.description: The ring slice with seq > after (after optional level/event-prefix filtering), oldest-first. Each entry is an RFC 0010 §3.2 log line VERBATIM with one added field, the ring `seq`.

event_line.description: One RFC 0010 §3.2 JSON-lines telemetry line plus the ring `seq`. OPEN: event-specific fields (tool, server, tokens_in/tokens_out, resource_uri, route, limit, value, cap, child_agent_id, child_path, depth, signal, finish_reason, result_bytes, ...) ride additionalProperties keyed by the event name — a consumer ignores fields it does not recognise.

seq.description: Monotonic ring sequence — the ONLY field added over the stderr line (RFC 0016 §7.2). The subscriber's cursor key; advance `after` to the largest seq seen.

ts.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp, millisecond precision. Always UTC, never local time.

event.description: Dotted event type — the primary index/filter key. A small CLOSED vocabulary (RFC 0010 §3.3, see $defs.event_name_v1) BUT additive-tolerant: a new event name is a minor change (RFC 0016 §8.2), so this is typed `string` and a consumer MUST tolerate an unrecognised name (provisional modelling choice: enforcing the enum would break additive tolerance; the frozen v1 set lives in $defs.event_name_v1 for codegen/reference only).

run_id.description: ULID for the whole invocation, stable across the tree (RFC 0010 §3.2).

agent_id.description: Emitting process id; the supervisor uses the reserved `sup`/`root`.

agent_path.description: Dotted tree path (`0`, `0.2`, `0.2.1`). The cheap superpower: scope a subtree by prefix, no backend join.

pid.description: OS process id — joins the log tree to the OS `pstree`.

span_id.description: W3C span id, 8 bytes as 16 lowercase hex. Present in-span (RFC 0010 §3.2).

parent_span_id.description: Parent span id, 8 bytes as 16 lowercase hex. Present in-span.

trace_id.description: W3C trace id, 16 bytes as 32 lowercase hex. Present when trace propagation is on (default). Correlates across pods (RFC 0010 §3.6).

dur_ms.description: Duration in milliseconds, present on `*.end`/`*.result` events.

err.description: Structured error, present on error events — NEVER a stringified stack (RFC 0010 §3.2).

msg.description: Optional short human string — never the structured payload.

event_name_v1.description: The frozen v1 closed event vocabulary (RFC 0010 §3.3): 19 supervisor/lifecycle + 8 agentic-loop = 27 names. Provided for codegen/reference; NOT enforced on the `event` field, because a new name is additive (RFC 0016 §8.2) and consumers must tolerate it. `comp:"mcp"` reuses these names; it introduces none. The report-write events (`report.written`/`report.write.fail`) are emitted by the reference impl (RFC 0016 §6.3/§8.4) and SHOULD be treated as additive members.
